message:
refactor(ui): introduce skin layer in FrankenContainer and refine NeuralPulse rendering

FrankenContainer previously applied overflow-hidden directly on its
outer div, which clipped both the decorative stitched texture AND the
FrankenBolt corner decorations. Bolts that intentionally extend beyond
the card boundary (via negative positioning like -left-1.5 -top-1.5)
were being cut off, defeating their visual purpose.

This commit introduces a two-layer architecture:
  - The outer container div loses overflow-hidden, allowing bolts and
    other deliberately-overflowing children to render beyond the border.
  - A new "Internal Skin Layer" div (absolute inset-0, overflow-hidden,
    rounded-[inherit]) is inserted as the first child to contain only
    the stitched texture background and NeuralPulse effect. These
    effects correctly remain clipped to the card boundary.
  - Stitched texture opacity increased from 0.03 to 0.05 for slightly
    more visible "Frankenstein skin" texture.

NeuralPulse rendering is also refined:
  - Switches from inline style={{ color }} + via-current (which relies
    on CSS currentColor inheritance) to a CSS custom property approach:
    the wrapper div sets --pulse-color via inline style, and the pulse
    lines reference it with via-[var(--pulse-color)]. This is more
    explicit and avoids color inheritance issues in nested contexts.
  - Pulse line thickness refined from h-1/w-1 (4px) to h-[1.5px]/
    w-[1.5px] for a sharper, more precise energy-trace appearance.
  - Blur reduced from blur-[3px] to blur-[2px] for crisper glow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
